projectDescription: Open Brain is a persistent AI memory infrastructure layer — one Supabase database with pgvector, one MCP server, any AI client — that lets Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and any other AI share the same searchable knowledge base.
keyFeatures: Single Supabase PostgreSQL + pgvector database as persistent AI memory
Remote MCP server deployed as Supabase Edge Function — no local servers
Four MCP tools: search_thoughts, list_thoughts, thought_stats, capture_thought
Semantic search via 1536-dimensional embeddings (openai/text-embedding-3-small via OpenRouter)
Auto-metadata extraction (type, topics, people, action items) on every captured thought
Content fingerprint deduplication with SHA-256 upsert function
Works with Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and any MCP client
Access key authentication via URL query param or x-brain-key header
Progressive extension learning path (6 curated builds)
Community recipes for bulk-importing data from Gmail, ChatGPT, Obsidian, X/Twitter, Instagram, and more
Reusable agent skill packs for Claude Code and similar AI clients
Community dashboards (SvelteKit and Next.js) with Supabase auth
publicApiSurface: MCP tool: list_thoughts(limit?, type?, topic?, person?, days?)
SQL function: match_thoughts(query_embedding, match_threshold, match_count, filter)
SQL table: thoughts (id, content, embedding, metadata, content_fingerprint, created_at, updated_at)
Auth: x-brain-key header or ?key= query parameter
